what_it_does: This orienteering ledger entry documents the Company's systemd user-unit control layer: it lists the ~20 unit files and company.target, describes what each key service does, how they are wired, the export-to-reindex automation circuit, the source copies in company/ops/systemd, and the live state (only bridge and embed-pplx currently active).
observations: Frontmatter marks this as a terrain-entry with register descriptive, relation external, status unconfirmed, and coverage 12/22 files read.
About 21 company-* units plus company.target live in /home/tim/.config/systemd/user.

Key services include company-bridge.service (:8770), company-canvas.service (:5173), company-embed-pplx.service (:8007), a session supervisor, multiple STT/TTS/voice units, and exporter/reindex timers.
The export timer writes Claude jsonl to ~/corpora/claude-sessions every 20 minutes; the reindex timer delta-embeds it into cache-company via :8007 about 5 minutes later.
Source copies of the units are kept at /home/tim/company/ops/systemd/.
As of 2026-06-26, company.target is disabled, no company timers are active, canvas is inactive, and only bridge (:8770) and embed-pplx (:8007) are running.
The stale state of corpora and cache-company is attributed to the inactive export/reindex loop.
standouts: The defined control stack is only partially running: 0 active timers and company.target disabled.
Only two services are currently active (bridge :8770 and embed-pplx :8007), while the export/reindex loop that feeds corpora/cache-company is stalled.
There are both installed units under ~/.config/systemd/user and source copies under company/ops/systemd/.
conventions: Uses terrain-entry YAML frontmatter with fields like aliases, coverage, git_remote, ports, launches, produced, depends-on, relates-to.
Sections follow a standard orienteering template: What it is, How it works, What it connects to, When/where, Notes/evidence.
concerns: Status is 'unconfirmed' even though a live state was verified on 2026-06-26.
Coverage is only 12 of 22 files, leaving 10 unit files unread.
Source copies and installed units could drift without an explicit sync mechanism.
The reason timers and company.target are inactive is not given in the file.
The entry notes the export/reindex loop is the root cause of stale downstream data but does not say why it stopped.
notes: This file is the systemd map for the Company stack; the key takeaway is that the automation loop (exporter/reindex) and canvas are down, leaving only the bridge and embedder running and downstream caches stale.
questions: Why are company-agent-sessions-exporter.timer and company-claude-sessions-reindex.timer inactive?
Why is company.target disabled and company-canvas.service inactive?
Are the source copies in /home/tim/company/ops/systemd/ the authoritative versions, and how are they synced to ~/.config/systemd/user?
What is in the 10 unread unit files?
What does the bridge drop-in company-bridge.service.d/wire.conf configure?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to be the Company's control layer that boots and runs its services; the file shows the units define that layer but are only partially active, so the 'boot and run' purpose is not currently realized.
apparent_themes: Automation loops and scheduled data pipelines
intention_signals: The entry seems intended to create an inspectable, evidence-based map of the systemd control plane, explicitly grounding declared unit semantics against live system state and flagging where the intended automation has stalled.
novelty: It treats ordinary systemd user units as a ledgered 'substrate' with both declared configuration semantics and a verified runtime-state narrative, and it tracks source mirrors separately from the active installed copies.
contribution: It contributes a precise inventory of systemd units, their ports, wiring, source locations, and a verified state note that the export/reindex automation is inactive—information a fused system would keep for diagnosing why corpora and cache-company are stale.
summary_for_embedding: company-systemd is an orienteering ledger entry describing the Company's systemd user-unit control layer, including ~20 units and company.target, ports 8770/5173/8007, the exporter-to-reindex timer circuit, mirrored source copies in company/ops/systemd, and the current partially-active state where timers and canvas are down.
intention_for_embedding: It was created to document and verify how the Company stack is booted and kept current via systemd, while recording that the export/reindex loop is currently stalled.
